stage.title: Test strategy
stage.objective: Turn the feature's acceptance criteria into a concrete, capability-aware Test Plan: classify every acceptance unit (BE-logic / full-stack / FE / infra) and map it to exactly ONE Test Plan row → {layer, driver, test path}, with every layer/driver/threshold derived from Stage 0's resolved capability signals. ZERO project-shaped value is hardcoded — no runner name, project name, port, path literal, or auth threshold; each is a resolved signal or a discovery var (`{{e2e_runner}}` / `{{fixture_command}}`). No FE-testable unit is ever silently skipped. This shard is SELF-CONTAINED: it renders a complete Test Plan from the Stage 0 context block alone, so F4 can wire it into the Stage 3 seam without edits.

substep.title: Consume Stage 0 context + enumerate acceptance units
substep.action: Read the Stage 0 emitted context block verbatim — do NOT re-probe the filesystem or re-run `tmux-cli rules resolve`; every signal is already resolved upstream.
Extract the SIGNALS fields by name: `has_database`, `has_frontend`, `frontend_mode`, `n_auth_flows`, `run_target`.

Extract `e2e_runner_available` from the context block — it is NOT a Signals field; Stage 0 probed it separately (Playwright/Cypress config presence) and carries it in the block. READ it here; never re-probe.

Extract TOPOLOGY: source roots, layer names, the resolved test roots, and the resolved runner / fixture commands (`{{e2e_runner}}` / `{{fixture_command}}` from discovery). These supply the per-row test path and driver command — never a literal.
Enumerate the feature's acceptance units from the prior stages' acceptance criteria. Each acceptance criterion that asserts one observable behavior is ONE acceptance unit to classify.

check.action: Emit an EMPTY Test Plan table plus a one-line note ("No acceptance units to classify — Test Plan intentionally empty."). Do NOT fabricate rows. Proceed without crashing.

substep.title: Classify each acceptance unit
substep.action: Tag each acceptance unit as exactly ONE class. Apply these definitions:
substep.rule: BE-logic = pure domain / application logic with no I/O (aggregates, value objects, domain services, application handlers).
infra = persistence or external-adapter behavior (repository implementation, DB mapping, migration, external service adapter).
FE = browser-surface behavior (UI rendering, client-side interaction) exercised through the frontend.
full-stack = an HTTP request → response that flows through the whole stack (controller → application → domain → infrastructure).

Every unit gets exactly one class — no unit is left unclassified, and no unit carries two classes. When a unit could read as two classes, pick the OUTERMOST observable surface (full-stack over BE-logic when an HTTP boundary is asserted; FE over full-stack when a browser surface is asserted).

substep.title: Select layer + driver (decision table)
substep.action: For each classified unit, select its {layer, driver} from this decision table. Every gate reads a resolved capability signal — never a literal.

decision-table.rule: BE-logic → Unit layer, TDD driver: write the failing unit test FIRST, drive red→green (test-first ordering per AGENTS.md). The failing unit test precedes the implementation; this is the test-first / TDD contract.

infra / persistence → Integration layer, real-dependency driver: exercise the real dependency — a real DB when `has_database` resolves true. If `has_database` is unknown, match conservatively and still route to Integration (the UNKNOWN-matches-conservatively convention).

full-stack OR FE behavior AND `has_frontend` true AND `e2e_runner_available` true → E2E layer, Playwright driver using the resolved `{{e2e_runner}}` (never a literal runner name). Reuse the auth `storageState` fixture when `n_auth_flows >= 1` — i.e. the emitted `n_auth_flows` count meets the manifest's `min_auth_flows` ≥ 1 threshold (the `frontend-auth` pack gate). When `n_auth_flows < 1`, run the E2E WITHOUT storageState reuse — emit no storageState fixture.

FE-testable unit with NO browser runner (`has_frontend` true AND `e2e_runner_available` false), OR any full-stack unit without a browser surface (`has_frontend` false) → E2E layer, API / HTTP-level E2E fallback driver. This fallback is EXPLICIT and recorded in the row with its reason — it is NEVER a silent skip.

substep.rule: No FE-testable behavior is ever dropped: when `e2e_runner_available` is false, the unit falls back to the HTTP-level driver — it does not vanish from the Test Plan.
storageState reuse is gated SOLELY on the resolved `n_auth_flows` count versus the `min_auth_flows` threshold; never assume auth flows exist.

substep.title: Emit the Test Plan
substep.action: Render a markdown Test Plan table — ONE row per acceptance unit — with columns: `| Acceptance unit | Class | Layer | Driver | Test path |`.

Resolve each row's Test path from the TOPOLOGY test roots (unit / integration / E2E roots) and each row's driver command from the resolved `{{e2e_runner}}` / `{{fixture_command}}` discovery vars — never a literal path, port, or runner name.

For every E2E row, state the driver explicitly: Playwright (when `e2e_runner_available`) or the HTTP-level fallback (when not). For Playwright rows with `n_auth_flows >= 1`, note storageState reuse; otherwise note no storageState reuse.
Hand the rendered Test Plan table to the Stage 3 docs output for the downstream stages to consume.

execution-rules.rule: PORTABILITY: every value in the Test Plan is a resolved capability signal or a discovery var (`{{e2e_runner}}` / `{{fixture_command}}` / resolved test roots) — NEVER a hardcoded runner name, project name, port, path literal, or auth threshold.
NO SILENT SKIP: an FE-testable unit with no browser runner falls back to API / HTTP-level E2E, explicitly recorded with its reason — it is never omitted from the Test Plan.
SELF-CONTAINED: this shard resolves nothing on its own — it consumes Stage 0's emitted context block (SIGNALS + `e2e_runner_available` + TOPOLOGY) and never re-probes the filesystem.
SIGNAL NAMING: the emitted count is `n_auth_flows`; the manifest gate is `min_auth_flows` — reference both correctly (compare the `n_auth_flows` count against the `min_auth_flows` ≥ 1 threshold).
execution-rules.error-reporting: On a genuine harness/infra/spec defect (an MCP tool or `tmux-cli` command that errored after its own retries, a contradictory/under-specified goal spec, or a validator-error-class condition), follow the shared `error-reporting` procedure in `tmux/task-report.xml` — compose + submit autonomously, honoring api.enabled / DEDUP / RECURSION-guard. NEVER on expected work failures (red validate cycle, supervisor pushback, NEED_INPUT, unwritten-code test red) or daemon-owned goal-failures.

llm.critical-rules: • AUTONOMOUS stage — resolve every decidable fork to the highest-quality in-scope option and record the rationale; never block on the user (mirrors feature.xml's autonomous-stage mandate). • PORTABILITY IS MANDATORY — no runner name, project name, port, path literal, or auth threshold is hardcoded; every value is a resolved signal or discovery var (mirrors feature.xml's portability mandate). • ONE Test Plan row per acceptance unit, each classified exactly once and mapped to exactly one {layer, driver, test path}. • BE-logic is test-first (TDD): the failing unit test precedes implementation, red→green. • Playwright E2E is gated on `has_frontend` AND `e2e_runner_available`; storageState reuse is gated on `n_auth_flows >= 1` (`min_auth_flows` threshold); otherwise fall back to HTTP-level E2E — never a silent skip. • Zero acceptance units → empty Test Plan + a note, never a crash.
